text: I feel a peculiar sense of anticipation in the dimly lit, silent corridor. The sterile, modern lines clash with the absence of human presence, leaving me alert but isolated. In the adjacent space, the "R+D" wall promises innovation and human connection, but under this hushed darkness, those ideals feel distant and oddly vulnerable. My mind wanders—do places hold memories of past energy, or is this emptiness itself a kind of presence?
